violations.text: Rodent droppings were observed on bottom front shelving, in storage closet, underneath some kitchen equipment, and the in bathroom. Staff cleaned droppings at time of inspection. Establishment shall continue to clean and monitor for rodent activity. -Establishment shall contact pest control service to do a full evaluation/assessment of the facility. -Proof of pest control service must be provided to inspector. New Violation. Correct By: 01/22/2026

At the time of inspection, Utensils in the front service area and the back prep area were observed stored in stagnant water between uses. Methods for proper storage of in-use utensils were reviewed at the time of inspection. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 08/06/2024

Soap was not available at the hand-washing sink in the kitchen and in the employee bathroom. Hand-washing sinks must be provided with a supply of hand cleaning liquid, powder, or bar soap. Soap provided at the time of inspection Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 08/06/2024

The hand-washing sinks in the front service area and the employee bathroom were lacking paper towels. Paper towels were supplied at the time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 08/06/2024

Food contact surfaces of equipment/utensils (slicer) were observed with an accumulation of food and debris. The food contact surfaces of equipment must be clean to sight and touch. Slicer was removed from service and set for proper wash/rinse/sanitizing at the time of inspection. Proper cleaning methods and frequency were discussed with the PIC at the time of inspection. New Violation. Correct By: 08/06/2024

At the time of inspection, Utensils in the front service area and the back prep area were observed stored in stagnant water between uses. Methods for proper storage of in-use utensils were reviewed at the time of inspection. New Violation. Correct By: 08/06/2024
All hand sinks were lacking proper signage. Establishment to add signage to hand sink designating them for handwashing only. New Violation. Correct By: 06/22/2023
The prep sink requires a splash guard on both sides to prevent potential contamination. New Violation. Correct By: 07/06/2023
